quiz_title: CMFAS Module 9A - Mock Exam Set A

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes a Backwardation market?
options: The spot price is higher than the futures price
The futures price is lower than the spot price
The futures price is equal to the spot price
The futures price is higher than the spot price
questions.correct_answer: The futures price is lower than the spot price

questions.question: To maintain the original level of risk exposure, a rebalancing is necessary by moving units from one fund to the other. This is known as _________.
Which of the following statement/s is true of Payment Netting?
options: Maximises the need for funds & securities to change hands
Minimises the likelihood every party receives what it should get

questions.question: A structured product that allows issuers to transfer a specific credit risk to 3rd party investors is a _________instrument?

John went long a silver futures contract at $3200. Broker set maintenance margin lower at $3000. Under what market circumstance would the broker issue a margin call to John at the end of the day's trading?
In the event of liquidation, which category of bond holders would be the last to be repaid?
SGX requires ETFs to meet certain liquidity requirements, which of the following is correctly stated?
options: Fund size must be at least S$20 million & at least 25% of funds total number of issued shares are held by at least 500 shareholders
Fund size must be at least S$20 million & at least 25% of funds total number of issued shares are held by at least 100 shareholders
Fund size must be at least S$750,000 & at least 80% of funds total number of issued shares are held by shareholders
questions.correct_answer: Fund size must be at least S$20 million & at least 25% of funds total number of issued shares are held by at least 500 shareholders

questions.question: A structured product ______.
options: Is simple to understand & carries lower risk
Is usually a combination of a Fixed-income instrument & Derivative
Is strictly a Fixed Income instrument
Is really an equity instrument that's pays a fixed dividend
questions.correct_answer: Is usually a combination of a Fixed-income instrument & Derivative

questions.question: In the world of finance, risk refers to
Reverse convertible bonds are constructed by using a bond and ______.
An investor may suffer a partial loss on his original investment amount, which risk/s may trigger that?
Which one of the following futures contracts would be settled in cash instead of physical delivery?

The STI Double Long Fund (2x) seeks to deliver twice the daily percentage change in the level of the Straits Times Index (STI). If the STI registers a 10% gain at the end of the day what would be the change in the value of this fund?
The number of times that a dollar of assets is reinvested in a given year. What is this ratio known as?
The January futures price for oil is US$120 per barrel, but the spot price is US$100 per barrel. Which of the following statements BEST describes this situation?
Which of the following regulatory instrument(s) must an insurer marketing structured ILPs comply with?
Under which report/document can you find the "market value of exposure to derivatives"?
Portfolio Bonds are often called Lifestyle Policies, which statement below best explains it?
options: To satisfy an individual's changing investment objectives during his lifetime.
His investment portfolio remains the same, but the fund manager changes
The portfolio includes an insurance element, which implies lifetime.
It means the returns to the portfolio are fixed for the entire lifetime.
questions.correct_answer: To satisfy an individual's changing investment objectives during his lifetime.

questions.question: There are several fees & charges incurred under a Portfolio Bond, which statement below is FALSE?
options: Mortality charges can be attached to Portfolio Bonds
Dealing Account can have a negative balance
A hard copy of the yearly statement is provided free of charge
Early withdrawal charge will be imposed by the fund manager for early withdrawals
questions.correct_answer: A hard copy of the yearly statement is provided free of charge

questions.question: Which of the following could affect the share price of a company, which in turn could affect the company's profits?
options: An increase in the Consumer Price Index

questions.question: What is the typical annual fund management fee, for a manager of an equity portfolio?
Which of the following statements is Incorrect about A Portfolio of Investments with an insurance element?
options: They allow policy owners to appoint managers of their portfolios within the insurer's platform
They offer a wide range of investment choices
They carry more risk when compared to conventional bonds
Provides a high amount of death benefit
questions.correct_answer: Provides a high amount of death benefit

questions.question: Jerry runs a garment factory producing cotton t-shirts for many well-known brands. He has been monitoring the price of raw cotton and notice it has been on an upward trend. Since he typically buys cotton from the farmers every 6 months, how can Jerry protect himself from a price increase?
options: Buy a put option on cotton

questions.question: Structured deposits are one of four common wrappers, all the following are TRUE except______?
options: Only banks can issue structured deposits
Bank usually guarantees a return of capital
They are included in the Deposit Insurance Scheme
Structured deposits incur lower administrative costs
questions.correct_answer: They are included in the Deposit Insurance Scheme

questions.question: Which one of the following scenarios best describes liquidity risk from an institution perspective?
options: Pays a high dividend to its shareholders annually
Share price of the institution has been slowly & steadily rising over the last 5 years

questions.question: A retail CIS is subject to concentration limits, for Single Entity Limit exposure is capped at

A structured product manager issues an equity-linked note with a par-value of $1000. Out of that 60% is channelled into a zero-coupon bond, while the remaining 40% is used as premium for call options. The structured product aims to provide a return of the capital portion to investors at maturity.

Assuming the zero-coupon bond matures at the same time as the structured product, what must the maturity value of the zero-coupon be in order to return the principal to the investor?
questions.reference: C1, S1.6 to S1.7, including the Illustration

questions.question: An investor looking for greater downside protection, in the event of a knock-out occurring would be best advised to invest in a __________.
Products designed to protect capital are designed for risk-adverse investors, which of the following is an example of this type of product?
Assets of a Structured ILP are held under the investment-linked sub-fund. Who owns the assets in this sub-fund?

James owns 100 shares of BRZ stock at $15, shortly after he went long on 100 BRZ puts for some downside protection. The BRZ puts has a strike price of $15 and James paid $1.50 premium for each put. Calculate gain/loss if BRZ stock were to fall to $7.

An investment-linked insurance life policy indicates the investment holdings as follows:

Total market value is $7,500,00. If the fund is faced with a high demand of withdrawals, what is the 
impact the fund will face?
options: Fund size is rather small, hence it is easy to liquidate to meet any withdrawal demands
The fund manager should recommend investors switch to other funds to minimize the withdrawals
It takes time for the fund to liquidate its investment holdings to meet the high demand of withdrawals, as such the policy holder may only get part of the cash he needs
Since the fund invests in OTC bonds, they are more liquid when compared to exchange traded bonds.
questions.correct_answer: It takes time for the fund to liquidate its investment holdings to meet the high demand of withdrawals, as such the policy holder may only get part of the cash he needs

questions.question: A fund manager manages a portfolio of Singapore stocks worth $3,000,000. It has a beta of 1.2 to the STI Index. He is expecting near term declines over the next 2 months and wants to hedge his portfolio. The STI is currently at 3000, & the March contract is priced at 3100. With a multiplier of $10 per index point, calculate the number of futures contracts that the fund manager must sell to hedge the value of his stock portfolio.
questions.reference:
C3, S2.42 to S2.45 including example
a

questions.question: An investor invests US$100,000 at an exchange rate of US$1.00 to SGD$1.20 in March. Three months later the exchange rate drops to US$1.00 to SGD$1.10. However the investment in US$ terms makes a gain of 20%. What is the investor gain/losses in SGD % terms?

XYZ Insurance Pte Limited had introduced an Investment-linked policy that invests in a variety of funds, including a structured fund called Nest Fund.

Below is an extract from the Product Highlights Sheet of Nest Fund:
WHAT ARE THE FEES AND CHARGES OF THIS INVESTMENT?
Payable directly by you

You must pay the following fees and charges to the Fund agent based on the full market value of your investment:
- If you subsequently wish to switch your investment to another fund by the same fund management company, a switching fee of 0.5% is payable.

A client had invested in Nest Fund and wishes to switch to another fund by the same fund management company. The current market value of the investment is S$50,000. Calculate the client's switching fee.
Person A purchase a structured product from company XYZ. Below are the past year returns. Which of the following statement is true?
options: There is a guaranteed return of at least 20%
Performance of XYZ is higher than the benchmark for the past 3 years
Performance of XYZ is lower than the benchmark since inception
questions.correct_answer: Performance of XYZ is higher than the benchmark for the past 3 years

questions.question: How often should a structured ILP sub-fund be valued?
When preparing semi-annual reports for each ILP sub-fund, the report should contain the performance of the ILP sub-fund and a comparison versus the benchmark covering the periods of :-
options: One year, three years & ten years
Three months, six months, one year, three years, five years, ten years & since inception
Three months, six months, one year & since inception
questions.correct_answer: Three months, six months, one year, three years, five years, ten years & since inception

questions.question:
Which document is prepared in a question & answer format, and contains the following questions?

Who is the sub-fund suitable for?
What are the key risks of this investment?
What are the fees & charges of this investment?
How often are valuations available?
What is the primary risk to the upside potential component of a structured product?
options: Credit worthiness of the bond issuer

questions.question: When a market is closed to foreign investors, structured products may be the only way to replicate the performance of the product , without directly investing in securities of the market. What is one such example?
Joan is bearish on stock XYZ, she normally would sell the stock at the open & buy back before the market closes. Which strategy below would be a safer alternative for Joan?
When investors of structured products suffer a kick-in or a knock-out, what are these investors exposed to?

Jamie decided to buy a call option on XYZ stock & paid a premium $2. The call exercise price is $20. Within a week, XYZ share price decreased from $25 to $22. What is the percentage change in the intrinsic value of the call option?
An option that can only be exercised on pre-determined dates is known as _________?

Mr Tan is thinking of investing $100,000 into a structured ILP with the following features:
Issuer = XYZ Insurance Company 
Tenure = 5 years
Maturity Value = initial capital amount

Annual pay-out = 
(a) Guaranteed payment of 1% of the single premium, OR
(b) 5% of the initial capital of every year if the price of all 5 stocks are equal or above 85% of their initial stock prices

Mr Tan mention to his financial advisor he likes the guaranteed payment, which statement below is correct advice from his financial advisor?
options: XYZ insurance company has to observed the Code on CIS since it applies to ILP funds as well, hence the guarantee is safe.
Mr Tan can obtain additional secondary guarantee from MAS, if he has any doubts.
Since stocks have a good chance of appreciating over a five year term, Mr Tan has nothing to fear
Guarantees are only as good as the financial strength of the guarantors, therefore Mr Tan should monitor the credit standing of the party providing the guarantee over these 5 years.
questions.correct_answer: Guarantees are only as good as the financial strength of the guarantors, therefore Mr Tan should monitor the credit standing of the party providing the guarantee over these 5 years.

questions.question: The most common swap whereby cash flows occurring on the same dates are netted is known as a ____________?
If two stocks move independently of one another, meaning the movements are completely random, they have a correlation of _____.
Portfolio bonds provide investors with flexibility & a wide range of investment choices, however they may not be suitable for which category of investors?
options: Investors who have a long term time frame
Investors who are in the high income tax brackets
Investors who prioritize investment return over protection
Investors who are happy to invest in one or two funds and don't need additional features.
questions.correct_answer: Investors who are happy to invest in one or two funds and don't need additional features.

questions.question: The share price of Microsoft is currently trading at US$250. Jason is bearish on Microsoft and decides to initiate a short CFD position. His broker quotes him $248 bid & $252 offer. Subsequently the market rises to US$270. Jason then scrambles to close his position immediately, and broker quotes him US$268 bid & US$272 offer. Assuming financing & commission charges are $3, calculate Jason gain/loss when he closes his position.
